# Escaped block constructs that must NOT become block nodes

\# but not a heading, this whole line is a paragraph starting with a literal hash.

\## also not a level-two heading even though it looks like one.

1\. not a list because the period after the number is escaped

2\) also not a list when the paren is escaped

\- not a bullet because the dash is escaped at line start

\* also not a bullet and not emphasis at line start

\+ not a bullet either with an escaped plus

\> not a blockquote because the angle bracket is escaped

A line ending in two trailing spaces but then more text \
and a literal backslash-newline that is not a hard break here.

# Inline emphasis traps with literal asterisks and underscores

The expression a * b * c * d is multiplication, not emphasis, because of the spaces.

Use snake_case like use_snake_case_here and total_count_value without italics.

A word like wild*card or foo*bar*baz in the middle should not turn into emphasis.

Literal \*not bold\* and \_not italic\_ and \*\*not strong\*\* must round-trip verbatim.

Mixed: 5*3=15 and 2*2*2=8 are products, but *this is real emphasis* and **this is real strong**.

Underscore intra_word_emphasis is disabled by GFM, so a_b_c stays plain.

Trailing emphasis edge: *foo bar* baz *qux

# Inline code spans and backtick fences with embedded backticks

A simple `code span with *asterisks* and _underscores_ that must not render`.

Code containing a backtick uses double delimiters: `` here is a ` backtick `` inside.

Triple for code with double: ``` a `` b ``` and then normal text.

Inline `#hashtag`, `1. not a list`, `- not a bullet`, and `| not | a | table |` all literal.

A span with leading and trailing spaces: `  padded  ` should keep one space trim rule.

Text then `unterminated backtick at end of paragraph and the line just ends

# GFM table with pipes, escapes, and alignment that must survive

| Name | Expression | Note |
|:-----|:----------:|-----:|
| pipe in cell | `a \| b` | escaped pipe literal |
| code pipe | a \| b \| c | three escaped pipes |
| emphasis | *not* `**this**` | mixed inline |
| backslash | path\\to\\file | double backslashes |
| brackets | [link](url) and ![img](src) | literal-ish |

After the table, a paragraph with a stray | pipe that is not in any table at all.

And a line | with | pipes | but | no | header | separator | so not a table.

# Brackets, angle brackets, links, and HTML-ish escaping

Price comparison: price is 5 < 6 and 7 > 4 and 3 <= 3 and x > y always.

Literal brackets \[not a link\] and \[also not\]\(not-a-url\) must stay text.

A real [link](https://example.com/path?q=1&r=2) next to a fake [link] with no target.

Autolink <https://example.com> and an email <user@example.com> inline.

An angle thing <notatag> and </closer> and a comparison a<b and c>d together.

Reference style [ref][1] and shorthand [foo] should preserve [1]: https://example.com

Image ![alt with ] bracket](img.png "title with \" quote").

# Backslash escapes and the full punctuation gauntlet

Every ASCII punctuation escaped: \! \" \# \$ \% \& \' \( \) \* \+ \, \- \. \/ \: \; \< \= \> \? \@ \[ \\ \] \^ \_ \` \{ \| \} \~

A literal backslash before a non-escapable char: \a \b \9 \z stays as backslash plus char.

Double backslash \\ then star \\* means literal backslash then literal star.

Windows path C:\Users\name\Documents\file.txt with no escaping intent.

Regex like /^\d+\.\d+$/ and a\.b\.c domain-looking escapes.

Trailing single backslash at end of line \

# Deeply nested marks

This sentence has ***bold and italic*** together, then ***~~all three: bold, italic, and strike~~*** combined into one run. Edge case: **bold with *italic inside* and back to bold** plus *italic with **bold inside** then italic* again. A triple nest: **_`code inside bold italic`_** should round-trip. And ~~strike with **bold** and *italic* mixed~~ ends the line.
## Highlight wrapping bold and friends

A highlight around bold: <mark>**fully bold highlighted**</mark> and a highlight around italic: <mark>*italic inside highlight*</mark>. Mixed: <mark>plain then **bold** then *italic* then `code`</mark> all inside one mark. Reverse nesting where bold wraps highlight: **leading <mark>highlighted middle</mark> trailing bold**. Also strike wrapping highlight: ~~before <mark>marked</mark> after~~.
### Underline wrapping a link, and links inside underline

An underline around a link: <u>[underlined link text](https://example.com)</u> in a sentence. A link whose text is itself underlined: [<u>underlined inside link</u>](https://example.com/path). Nested both ways: <u>prefix [link](https://a.test) suffix</u> and [pre <u>under</u> post](https://b.test). Underline wrapping bold link: <u>**[bold underlined link](https://c.test)**</u>.
#### Links with formatted text

A link whose text is bold: [**bold link**](https://example.com). Italic link text: [*italic link*](https://example.com). Strike link text: [~~struck link~~](https://example.com). Code in link text: [`code() link`](https://example.com). Mixed inside one link: [**bold** and *italic* and `code` in one anchor](https://example.com/mixed). Highlighted link text: [<mark>marked link</mark>](https://example.com). A reference-style [formatted **ref** link][ref1].

[ref1]: https://example.com/reference
##### Inline code adjacent to bold and other marks

No space here: `code`**bold** stuck together, and **bold**`code` reversed. Word-attached: pre`code`post then a`code`b. Bold then code then italic with no gaps: **b**`c`*i*. Code containing markdown that must stay literal: `**not bold** *not italic* ~~not strike~~ [not a](link)`. Code containing backtick-looking content: `` use ` backtick `` inside. Double adjacency: ``**``**bold after double-tick code**.
###### Literal angle-bracket tags typed as prose

The user literally wrote the string <mark> and the string <u> as plain prose, and they must NOT become real marks. For example: "To highlight text, wrap it in <mark>...</mark> tags" — but here the closing should also stay literal: type <u> to start an underline and </u> to end it. Tricky: a real highlight <mark>followed by a literal mention of <mark> as text</mark>. Escaped variants: \<mark> and \<u> should render as visible angle-bracket text. HTML-entity form: <mark> and <u> are also just prose.
Adjacent same-mark spans that should merge or stay separate

**bold one****bold two** are two adjacent bold spans with no gap and should visually merge. With a space they stay distinct: **bold A** **bold B**. Italic adjacency: *a**b* where the inner is ambiguous. Highlight adjacency: <mark>first</mark><mark>second</mark> touching, versus <mark>first</mark> <mark>second</mark> separated. Strike touching strike: ~~x~~~~y~~ — note the four tildes in the middle. Underline touching: <u>p</u><u>q</u>.
Emphasis at word boundaries and intraword edge cases

Intraword emphasis should NOT trigger with underscores: snake_case_variable and foo_bar_baz stay literal. But asterisks can: un**frigging**believable and wi*ki*pedia. Leading/trailing boundary: *start of line italic and **bold at end**. Punctuation hugging: **bold!**, (*parenthesized italic*), "**quoted bold**". Possessive after code: the `array`'s length. Numbers: 2*3*4 versus 2 * 3 * 4. Unicode boundary: café**bold**über.
Links with titles and parentheses in URLs

Link with a title: [titled link](https://example.com "This is the title"). Title containing quotes: [tricky](https://example.com "He said \"hi\""). Parentheses in URL needing angle brackets: [wiki](<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Markdown_(markup_language)>). Bare-ish parens URL: [paren url](https://example.com/path_(with_parens)). URL with query and fragment: [query](https://example.com/search?q=a+b&x=1#frag "Search title"). Formatted text plus title plus parens: [**bold** <mark>marked</mark> *italic*](https://example.com/foo_(bar)/baz "All together now").
Combination stress: everything in one paragraph

Begin with <u>underlined [a **bold** link](https://ex.com/a_(b) "t")</u>, then <mark>a highlight wrapping **bold *and italic* and ~~strike~~** plus `code`</mark>, then a literal mention that you can type <mark> and <u> as text without them activating, then adjacent **x****y** bold, then code-touching-bold `f()`**g** with no space, then a link with formatted text and a parenthesized title-bearing URL [<mark>**deep**</mark>](<https://ex.com/p_(q)/r> "final (title)") to close.

# Fenced Code Blocks No language: ``` plain text, no highlighting indented line ``` With language: ```python def hello(): return "world" ``` With info string and extra args: ```javascript {.line-numbers highlight=2} const x = 1 const y = 2 ``` Uppercase and odd language tag: ```C++ int main() { return 0; } ``` Tilde-fenced (CommonMark allows ~~~): ~~~ruby puts "tildes fence this" ~~~ # Code Content That Looks Like Markdown ```markdown # This is NOT a real heading - This is NOT a real list item - > This is NOT a real blockquote | not | a | table | |-----|---|-------| | a | b | c | --- **not bold** and *not italic* and `not inline code` [not a link](http://example.com) ``` The code block above must be preserved verbatim, including the blank lines and the thematic break inside it. # Backticks Inside Code A fenced block whose content contains triple backticks requires a longer fence: ````markdown Here is a nested fence: ```python print("hello") ``` And a lone ``` on its own line. ```` Inline code with embedded backticks: `` a ` b `` and ``` `` double `` ```. An even longer outer fence: `````text ```` ``` `` ` ````` # Code Blocks With Blank Lines And Trailing Whitespace ``` first line third line after a blank sixth line after two blanks trailing spaces here leading tab here ``` Indented code block (4 spaces), which also contains blank-looking lines: line one of indented code line three after blank deeper indent preserved Text resumes here as a normal paragraph. # Blockquotes: Single, Multi-line, Nested, And With Block Children > A single-line blockquote. > A blockquote that spans > multiple lines using lazy > continuation across rows. > Level one > > Level two nested > > > Level three nested > back to level one > Blockquote containing a list and code: > > 1. first > 2. second > > ```python > x = 42 > ``` > > And a nested quote with a heading: > > ## Quoted heading > > Final quoted line. # GFM Tables: Empty Cells, Escaped Pipes, Alignment, Inline Formatting | Left | Center | Right | Default | |:-----|:------:|------:|---------| | a | b | c | d | | | | | | | `code` | **bold** | *italic* | [link](http://x.io) | | a \| b | c \| d | e\|f | escaped \| pipes | | ~~strike~~ | line
break | 100% | emoji 🚀 | A table with ragged rows (fewer and more cells than the header): | H1 | H2 | H3 | |----|----|----| | only one | | one | two | three | four extra | | x | y | z | Minimal two-column table with all alignment markers in one row: | :--- | :--: | ---: | |------|------|------| | L | C | R | # Thematic Breaks, Headings 1-6, And Trailing Hashes Three styles of thematic break, all valid: --- *** ___ Spaced and longer variants: - - - * * * _______________ # Heading level 1 ## Heading level 2 ### Heading level 3 #### Heading level 4 ##### Heading level 5 ###### Heading level 6 ## Heading with trailing hashes ## ### Trailing hashes with trailing space ### #### Closing sequence of different length # Not a heading (no space after hash): #notaheading Seventh-level is not a heading: ####### Still a paragraph starting with hashes Setext heading level 1 ====================== Setext heading level 2 ---------------------- # Images: Standalone And Inline Within Sentences Standalone image as its own block (often becomes a figure): ![A standalone alt text](https://example.com/standalone.png "Optional title") Inline image within a sentence: here is ![inline icon](https://example.com/icon.png) sitting between words, followed by more text. Multiple images in one paragraph: ![first](https://example.com/1.png) and ![second](https://example.com/2.png "second title") right next to each other. Linked image (image wrapped in a link): [![clickable](https://example.com/thumb.png)](https://example.com/full) Reference-style image: ![ref alt][img-ref] Image with empty alt and special chars in URL: ![](https://example.com/path%20with%20spaces.png?a=1&b=2) Image with brackets and pipes in alt: ![alt [with] brackets | and pipe](https://example.com/odd.png) [img-ref]: https://example.com/reference.png "Reference title"

# Long soft-wrapped paragraph

This is one single logical paragraph that has been soft-wrapped across many
physical lines using single newlines that should be joined into one flowing
paragraph by a CommonMark parser because a single line break inside a
paragraph is treated as a space rather than a hard break and the round trip
converter must decide whether to preserve these soft wraps or collapse them
into one long line and either way the meaning of the text should remain
identical across the conversion and back again and we keep going to make sure
the line is genuinely long enough to exercise wrapping logic at eighty columns
and beyond and beyond and beyond until the very end of the thought finally
arrives here.
